name: Kawela Bay
desc: Ultra-exclusive, ultra-remote — about 15 homes, mile from nearest neighbors, surviving private enclave.
insider: Kawela Bay beach is public access but almost nobody knows — walk the coastal trail past Turtle Bay Resort's golf course
Sea turtle nesting happens on this beach from May through October — stay quiet and keep your distance
This is the quietest stretch of North Shore coastline — bring a book, snorkel gear, and plan to stay all day
The old WWII pillbox bunker ruins along the coast are an eerie hidden attraction from the island's wartime past
Homes here sell for $5M-$20M+ when they rarely come on the market — there are only about 15 in the entire enclave
The bay is naturally sheltered and calm even when the rest of the North Shore has massive winter surf
Turtle Bay Resort next door has restaurants, golf, and amenities that serve as the de facto neighborhood center
The coastal trail from Turtle Bay through Kawela Bay is one of the most beautiful flat walks on Oahu
Lost and other TV shows filmed extensively along this coast — the untouched shoreline looks cinematic for a reason
There is zero commercial anything in Kawela Bay — nearest grocery is Kahuku or Haleiwa, both 15+ minutes away
